---
title: "Parallel Computing"
sidebar: false
toc: false
---

[← All courses](../teaching.qmd){.back-link}

::: {.page-intro}
A new course on **high-performance computing in Julia**, aimed at third-year **ENSAI** students who
are comfortable in Python but new to Julia. The guiding idea: speed doesn't come from a "magic"
language but from understanding what the machine actually does — cost per operation, data movement,
and sharing. **Python is the familiar point of comparison; Julia is the test bench.**
:::

::: {.callout-note title="In preparation"}
This course is being developed and has not been taught yet. The materials linked below are early
drafts and will evolve.
:::

A single **red thread** runs through the course — estimating **π by Monte-Carlo** — carried from a
plain sequential loop, to multithreading, to the GPU.

## Modules

```{=html}
<div class="aurora-card-grid">
  <div class="aurora-card">
    <span class="aurora-card__icon">🧩</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__title">1 · Compilation &amp; Types</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__desc">How Julia specialises code on types — why a pure Python loop takes ~1&nbsp;s where Julia runs in milliseconds. Tools: <code>@code_warntype</code>, <code>@btime</code>; Python bytecode via <code>dis</code>.</span>
  </div>
  <div class="aurora-card">
    <span class="aurora-card__icon">🔀</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__title">2 · Multiple Dispatch vs OOP</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__desc">Julia's multiple dispatch as a language idea, contrasted with Python's OOP / <code>singledispatch</code>. A language module — no parallelism yet.</span>
  </div>
  <div class="aurora-card">
    <span class="aurora-card__icon">🧵</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__title">3 · Threads &amp; Concurrency</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__desc"><code>@threads</code>, race conditions, and Python's GIL (threads vs processes). Monte-Carlo π, multithreaded.</span>
  </div>
  <div class="aurora-card">
    <span class="aurora-card__icon">⚡</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__title">4 · Memory &amp; GPU</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__desc">The memory hierarchy and locality, batching, and the GPU (CUDA; PyTorch CPU vs CUDA). Estimating π on the GPU.</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Approach

Each module pairs a **common Python track** (zero-install, in Jupyter/Colab — so nobody falls
behind) with an optional **Julia deep-dive** (`@btime`, `@threads`, the GPU). The teaching engine is
comparing the *numbers* between the two: a Python loop at ~1&nbsp;s next to a Julia loop in
milliseconds makes the cost of abstraction concrete. Slides frame each session; the Pluto notebooks
are live, executable demos.

## Materials

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Slides

::: {.resource-grid}
[Course Slides (draft)](slides/cours.qmd){.resource-chip}
:::

## Pluto Notebooks

::: {.resource-grid}
[1 — Compilation &amp; Types](pluto/1_compilation_et_types.jl){.resource-chip}
[2 — Dispatch vs OOP](pluto/2_dispatch_vs_poo.jl){.resource-chip}
[3 — Threads &amp; Concurrency](pluto/3_threads_et_concurrence.jl){.resource-chip}
[4 — Memory &amp; GPU](pluto/4_memoire_et_gpu.jl){.resource-chip}
:::

## Related

::: {.resource-grid}
[Introduction to GPU Architecture](../../coding/parallel_computing_lectures/gpu_lecture.qmd){.resource-chip}
:::

:::
